light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Toccoa River offers fair to good trout fishing conditions with a recent stocking boosting angler success. Expect trout to feed comfortably near cover in runs, seams, and structure.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is best due to low rain probability, moderate sky cover, and calm conditions supporting a balanced nymph approach and comfortable trout positioning.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current flow is 227 cfs, gage height is 1.2 ft, but USGS water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Skies are partly cloudy with about 30% cover, pressure is steady at 30.08 inHg with no strong trend, wind averages 3.5 mph, and light rain is possible later.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor runs, seams, and structure near cover, feeding cautiously under moderate light. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell nymphs for best results.
